items.title: Activist Investors Target Food Companies as Growth Slows. Lamb Weston Is the Latest.
items.description: Activists target companies with strong brands but lagging stocks, seeking cost cuts, portfolio pruning, or corporate breakups.

items.title: McDonald’s offers customers free meal in exchange for this one thing
items.description: Starting March 6, McDonald’s is offering customers a free meal in exchange for a confession. The fast food giant launched its “First Job Confessional” campaign, a reality TV-inspired promotional series, “where fans can dish out their first job stories and the skills they picked up along the ...

items.title: McDonald's Big Arch Burger Tests Brand Power And Rich Valuation
items.description: McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) has introduced the Big Arch burger as part of an intensifying fast food "burger wars" environment. The launch is supported by prominent social media marketing and viral appearances from the McDonald's CEO. Rivalry with competitors such as Burger King and Wendy's has been amplified through playful online exchanges around the new burger. McDonald's has also rolled out a "First Job Confessional" campaign to deepen customer and worker engagement through personal...

items.title: 3 Surprising Stocks That Hit Fresh Highs Last Week
items.description: Some consumer discretionary stocks are rallying while the market is sliding.

items.title: Dutch Bros Expands Food Program: Can it Lift Ticket and Traffic?
items.description: BROS pushes deeper into food, expanding to 300+ stores with a 2026 systemwide goal as early tests show higher tickets, transactions and nearly 4% comp sales lift.
